channel.title: Internship Worklog on AWS Portfolio Workshop
channel.description: Recent content in Internship Worklog on AWS Portfolio Workshop

item.title: Week 1
item.description:
🎯 Week 1 objectives

  • Learn AWS Vietnam’s history, culture, internship rules and work processes.
  • Create, verify and secure a personal AWS account for practice.
  • Prepare the IDE, AWS CLI and architecture design tools.

📅 Work completed during the week

No.TaskStart dateCompletion dateReferences
1Learn AWS Vietnam’s history, culture, internship rules and work processes.05/05/202605/05/2026AWS Documentation
2Create, verify and secure a personal AWS account for practice.06/05/202606/05/2026AWS Documentation
3Prepare the IDE, AWS CLI and architecture design tools.07/05/202607/05/2026AWS Documentation
4Learn AWS Vietnam’s history, culture, internship rules and work processes.08/05/202608/05/2026AWS Documentation
5Create, verify and secure a personal AWS account for practice.09/05/202609/05/2026AWS Documentation

🏆 Achievements

  • Learn AWS Vietnam’s work culture and security requirements; prepare a complete AWS practice environment.
  • Completed the planned practical activities and documented the weekly results.

💡 Knowledge gained

  • Strengthened AWS knowledge and system deployment skills through the GreenLens Kids roadmap.
  • Improved skills in analysis, testing, technical documentation and presentation.

item.title: Week 2
item.description:
🎯 Week 2 objectives

  • Practice using the AWS Management Console and basic workshops.
  • Learn how to write a standardized Lab Guide with screenshots.
  • Draw AWS architecture and data-flow diagrams with draw.io.

📅 Work completed during the week

No.TaskStart dateCompletion dateReferences
1Practice using the AWS Management Console and basic workshops.11/05/202611/05/2026AWS Documentation
2Learn how to write a standardized Lab Guide with screenshots.12/05/202612/05/2026AWS Documentation
3Draw AWS architecture and data-flow diagrams with draw.io.13/05/202613/05/2026AWS Documentation
4Study IAM, S3, Lambda, API Gateway, DynamoDB, CloudWatch and Cognito.14/05/202614/05/2026AWS Documentation
5Summarize the foundation for the GreenLens Kids project.15/05/202615/05/2026AWS Documentation
6Practice using the AWS Management Console and basic workshops.undefinedundefinedAWS Documentation
7Learn how to write a standardized Lab Guide with screenshots.undefinedundefinedAWS Documentation

🏆 Achievements

  • Understand core AWS services and present technical knowledge through a clear Lab Guide.
  • Completed the planned practical activities and documented the weekly results.

💡 Knowledge gained

  • Strengthened AWS knowledge and system deployment skills through the GreenLens Kids roadmap.
  • Improved skills in analysis, testing, technical documentation and presentation.

item.title: Week 3
item.description:
🎯 Week 3 objectives

  • Analyze the problem, functions and technical requirements of GreenLens Kids.
  • Study Amazon S3 and the Object Storage model.
  • Practice Amazon EBS and the Block Storage model.

📅 Work completed during the week

No.TaskStart dateCompletion dateReferences
1Analyze the problem, functions and technical requirements of GreenLens Kids.18/05/202618/05/2026AWS Documentation
2Study Amazon S3 and the Object Storage model.19/05/202619/05/2026AWS Documentation
3Practice Amazon EBS and the Block Storage model.20/05/202620/05/2026AWS Documentation
4Practice Amazon EFS and the File Storage model.21/05/202621/05/2026AWS Documentation
5Compare cost and performance and select a suitable storage solution.22/05/202622/05/2026AWS Documentation
6Analyze the problem, functions and technical requirements of GreenLens Kids.undefinedundefinedAWS Documentation
7Study Amazon S3 and the Object Storage model.undefinedundefinedAWS Documentation

🏆 Achievements

  • Differentiate S3, EBS and EFS; finalize project requirements and the storage direction.
  • Completed the planned practical activities and documented the weekly results.

💡 Knowledge gained

  • Strengthened AWS knowledge and system deployment skills through the GreenLens Kids roadmap.
  • Improved skills in analysis, testing, technical documentation and presentation.

item.title: Week 4
item.description:
🎯 Week 4 objectives

  • Create the overall architecture draft in draw.io.
  • Model interactions between API Gateway, Lambda, S3, DynamoDB and Cognito.
  • Define data flows and integration points between components.

📅 Work completed during the week

No.TaskStart dateCompletion dateReferences
1Create the overall architecture draft in draw.io.25/05/202625/05/2026AWS Documentation
2Model interactions between API Gateway, Lambda, S3, DynamoDB and Cognito.26/05/202626/05/2026AWS Documentation
3Define data flows and integration points between components.27/05/202627/05/2026AWS Documentation
4Evaluate architecture validity, availability and latency.28/05/202628/05/2026AWS Documentation
5Review security and finalize the system design documentation.29/05/202629/05/2026AWS Documentation
6Create the overall architecture draft in draw.io.undefinedundefinedAWS Documentation
7Model interactions between API Gateway, Lambda, S3, DynamoDB and Cognito.undefinedundefinedAWS Documentation

🏆 Achievements

  • Complete the Serverless architecture diagram and design documentation for development.
  • Completed the planned practical activities and documented the weekly results.

💡 Knowledge gained

  • Strengthened AWS knowledge and system deployment skills through the GreenLens Kids roadmap.
  • Improved skills in analysis, testing, technical documentation and presentation.

item.title: Week 5
item.description:
🎯 Week 5 objectives

  • Configure IAM Users, Groups, Roles and Custom Policies using Least Privilege.
  • Create and configure an S3 Bucket for GreenLens Kids images.
  • Configure S3 access permissions, CORS and security policies.

📅 Work completed during the week

No.TaskStart dateCompletion dateReferences
1Configure IAM Users, Groups, Roles and Custom Policies using Least Privilege.01/06/202601/06/2026AWS Documentation
2Create and configure an S3 Bucket for GreenLens Kids images.02/06/202602/06/2026AWS Documentation
3Configure S3 access permissions, CORS and security policies.03/06/202603/06/2026AWS Documentation
4Manage AWS resources with the AWS CLI.04/06/202604/06/2026AWS Documentation
5Set up basic monitoring and logging with CloudWatch.05/06/202605/06/2026AWS Documentation
6Configure IAM Users, Groups, Roles and Custom Policies using Least Privilege.undefinedundefinedAWS Documentation
7Create and configure an S3 Bucket for GreenLens Kids images.undefinedundefinedAWS Documentation

🏆 Achievements

  • Configure the IAM, S3, AWS CLI and CloudWatch foundation for the project.
  • Completed the planned practical activities and documented the weekly results.

💡 Knowledge gained

  • Strengthened AWS knowledge and system deployment skills through the GreenLens Kids roadmap.
  • Improved skills in analysis, testing, technical documentation and presentation.

item.title: Week 6
item.description:
🎯 Week 6 objectives

  • Develop Lambda Functions for business logic.
  • Design RESTful APIs with Amazon API Gateway.
  • Configure API Gateway integration with Lambda.

📅 Work completed during the week

No.TaskStart dateCompletion dateReferences
1Develop Lambda Functions for business logic.08/06/202608/06/2026AWS Documentation
2Design RESTful APIs with Amazon API Gateway.09/06/202609/06/2026AWS Documentation
3Configure API Gateway integration with Lambda.10/06/202610/06/2026AWS Documentation
4Process JSON data between the client and backend.11/06/202611/06/2026AWS Documentation
5Test HTTP requests and responses and finalize the endpoints.12/06/202612/06/2026AWS Documentation
6Develop Lambda Functions for business logic.undefinedundefinedAWS Documentation
7Design RESTful APIs with Amazon API Gateway.undefinedundefinedAWS Documentation

🏆 Achievements

  • Build and successfully test the Serverless backend with Lambda and API Gateway.
  • Completed the planned practical activities and documented the weekly results.

💡 Knowledge gained

  • Strengthened AWS knowledge and system deployment skills through the GreenLens Kids roadmap.
  • Improved skills in analysis, testing, technical documentation and presentation.

item.title: Week 7
item.description:
🎯 Week 7 objectives

  • Study the NoSQL data model for GreenLens Kids.
  • Design tables, Partition Keys and Sort Keys in DynamoDB.
  • Deploy tables and configure data attributes.

📅 Work completed during the week

No.TaskStart dateCompletion dateReferences
1Study the NoSQL data model for GreenLens Kids.15/06/202615/06/2026AWS Documentation
2Design tables, Partition Keys and Sort Keys in DynamoDB.16/06/202616/06/2026AWS Documentation
3Deploy tables and configure data attributes.17/06/202617/06/2026AWS Documentation
4Integrate the AWS SDK with Lambda for CRUD operations.18/06/202618/06/2026AWS Documentation
5Create a GSI and test data-query performance.19/06/202619/06/2026AWS Documentation
6Study the NoSQL data model for GreenLens Kids.undefinedundefinedAWS Documentation
7Design tables, Partition Keys and Sort Keys in DynamoDB.undefinedundefinedAWS Documentation

🏆 Achievements

  • Master key and index design and CRUD operations on DynamoDB with the AWS SDK.
  • Completed the planned practical activities and documented the weekly results.

💡 Knowledge gained

  • Strengthened AWS knowledge and system deployment skills through the GreenLens Kids roadmap.
  • Improved skills in analysis, testing, technical documentation and presentation.

item.title: Week 8
item.description:
🎯 Week 8 objectives

  • Create and configure a Cognito User Pool for registration and login.
  • Study the Identity Pool and AWS resource authorization.
  • Integrate a Cognito Authorizer with API Gateway.

📅 Work completed during the week

No.TaskStart dateCompletion dateReferences
1Create and configure a Cognito User Pool for registration and login.22/06/202622/06/2026AWS Documentation
2Study the Identity Pool and AWS resource authorization.23/06/202623/06/2026AWS Documentation
3Integrate a Cognito Authorizer with API Gateway.24/06/202624/06/2026AWS Documentation
4Test JWT tokens and API access permissions.25/06/202625/06/2026AWS Documentation
5Configure S3 access for authenticated users.26/06/202626/06/2026AWS Documentation
6Create and configure a Cognito User Pool for registration and login.undefinedundefinedAWS Documentation
7Study the Identity Pool and AWS resource authorization.undefinedundefinedAWS Documentation

🏆 Achievements

  • Protect APIs with Cognito and JWT and apply user-based resource permissions.
  • Completed the planned practical activities and documented the weekly results.

💡 Knowledge gained

  • Strengthened AWS knowledge and system deployment skills through the GreenLens Kids roadmap.
  • Improved skills in analysis, testing, technical documentation and presentation.

item.title: Week 9
item.description:
🎯 Week 9 objectives

  • Centralize API Gateway, Lambda and DynamoDB logs in CloudWatch Logs.
  • Configure Metrics, CloudWatch Alarms and a Dashboard.
  • Monitor performance and identify bottlenecks and errors.

📅 Work completed during the week

No.TaskStart dateCompletion dateReferences
1Centralize API Gateway, Lambda and DynamoDB logs in CloudWatch Logs.29/06/202629/06/2026AWS Documentation
2Configure Metrics, CloudWatch Alarms and a Dashboard.30/06/202630/06/2026AWS Documentation
3Monitor performance and identify bottlenecks and errors.01/07/202601/07/2026AWS Documentation
4Use Logs Insights and AWS X-Ray to find root causes.02/07/202602/07/2026AWS Documentation
5Configure automatic alerts through Amazon SNS and handle incidents.03/07/202603/07/2026AWS Documentation
6Centralize API Gateway, Lambda and DynamoDB logs in CloudWatch Logs.undefinedundefinedAWS Documentation
7Configure Metrics, CloudWatch Alarms and a Dashboard.undefinedundefinedAWS Documentation

🏆 Achievements

  • Complete monitoring, alerting and incident-handling processes for GreenLens Kids.
  • Completed the planned practical activities and documented the weekly results.

💡 Knowledge gained

  • Strengthened AWS knowledge and system deployment skills through the GreenLens Kids roadmap.
  • Improved skills in analysis, testing, technical documentation and presentation.

item.title: Week 10
item.description:
🎯 Week 10 objectives

  • Perform end-to-end testing of the GreenLens Kids system.
  • Optimize Lambda, APIs and DynamoDB to reduce response time.
  • Review S3 and API access permissions and security.

📅 Work completed during the week

No.TaskStart dateCompletion dateReferences
1Perform end-to-end testing of the GreenLens Kids system.06/07/202606/07/2026AWS Documentation
2Optimize Lambda, APIs and DynamoDB to reduce response time.07/07/202607/07/2026AWS Documentation
3Review S3 and API access permissions and security.08/07/202608/07/2026AWS Documentation
4Analyze costs and apply AWS Cost Optimization practices.09/07/202609/07/2026AWS Documentation
5Evaluate test results and finalize the deployment configuration.10/07/202610/07/2026AWS Documentation
6Perform end-to-end testing of the GreenLens Kids system.undefinedundefinedAWS Documentation
7Optimize Lambda, APIs and DynamoDB to reduce response time.undefinedundefinedAWS Documentation

🏆 Achievements

  • Complete system testing and improve performance, security and operating costs.
  • Completed the planned practical activities and documented the weekly results.

💡 Knowledge gained

  • Strengthened AWS knowledge and system deployment skills through the GreenLens Kids roadmap.
  • Improved skills in analysis, testing, technical documentation and presentation.

item.title: Week 11
item.description:
🎯 Week 11 objectives

  • Complete the remaining GreenLens Kids features and lock the source code.
  • Verify system flows and capture evidence of AWS resources.
  • Record a demo video of the main system functions.

📅 Work completed during the week

No.TaskStart dateCompletion dateReferences
1Complete the remaining GreenLens Kids features and lock the source code.13/07/202613/07/2026AWS Documentation
2Verify system flows and capture evidence of AWS resources.14/07/202614/07/2026AWS Documentation
3Record a demo video of the main system functions.15/07/202615/07/2026AWS Documentation
4Update the official architecture diagram to match the real infrastructure.16/07/202616/07/2026AWS Documentation
5Complete the Technical Documentation and report content.17/07/202617/07/2026AWS Documentation
6Complete the remaining GreenLens Kids features and lock the source code.undefinedundefinedAWS Documentation
7Verify system flows and capture evidence of AWS resources.undefinedundefinedAWS Documentation

🏆 Achievements

  • Package the demo product, architecture diagram and technical documentation for the report.
  • Completed the planned practical activities and documented the weekly results.

💡 Knowledge gained

  • Strengthened AWS knowledge and system deployment skills through the GreenLens Kids roadmap.
  • Improved skills in analysis, testing, technical documentation and presentation.

item.title: Week 12
item.description:
🎯 Week 12 objectives

  • Review and remove test AWS resources to prevent additional charges.
  • Complete the internship report, Worklog and company confirmation documents.
  • Review all documentation, screenshots and practice results.

📅 Work completed during the week

No.TaskStart dateCompletion dateReferences
1Review and remove test AWS resources to prevent additional charges.20/07/202620/07/2026AWS Documentation
2Complete the internship report, Worklog and company confirmation documents.21/07/202621/07/2026AWS Documentation
3Review all documentation, screenshots and practice results.22/07/202622/07/2026AWS Documentation
4Design slides and rehearse the presentation.23/07/202623/07/2026AWS Documentation
5Summarize lessons learned and the Cloud Engineering/DevOps career direction.24/07/202624/07/2026AWS Documentation
6Review and remove test AWS resources to prevent additional charges.undefinedundefinedAWS Documentation
7Complete the internship report, Worklog and company confirmation documents.undefinedundefinedAWS Documentation

🏆 Achievements

  • Complete the report, Worklog and slides and prepare for the internship defense.
  • Completed the planned practical activities and documented the weekly results.

💡 Knowledge gained

  • Strengthened AWS knowledge and system deployment skills through the GreenLens Kids roadmap.
  • Improved skills in analysis, testing, technical documentation and presentation.
